old_value: {"id":"smallhold-9ouq.75","title":"handle_like and handle_announce create duplicate notifications","description":"In inbox.rs, handle_like (line 1264) and handle_announce (line 1326) use plain INSERT INTO notifications without OR IGNORE and without checking if a notification already exists for the same (account_id, kind, from_remote_account_id, post_id) tuple. If a remote server resends the same Like or Announce activity (retries, software bugs, or malicious intent), duplicate notifications are created each time. This differs from the handle_create DM path (line 977) which correctly checks for existing notifications before inserting. Both handlers should use INSERT OR IGNORE with a UNIQUE constraint, or check for existing rows like the DM code does.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-07-19T22:49:40.621269241Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-19T22:49:40.621269241Z","labels":["correctness","review"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"smallhold-9ouq.75","depends_on_id":"smallhold-9ouq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-19T22:49:40.654786803Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value: {"description":"In inbox.rs, handle_like (line 1264) and handle_announce (line 1326) use plain INSERT INTO notifications without OR IGNORE. The notifications table has a unique dedup index (idx_notifications_dedup) on (account_id, kind, from_account_id, from_remote_account_id, post_id, remote_post_id). When a remote server resends the same Like or Announce (retries, software bugs), the INSERT violates the UNIQUE constraint, causing an unhandled sqlx error that propagates to the HTTP response as a 500 Internal Server Error. The remote server then interprets this as a transient failure and retries, creating a retry loop. The fix is to use INSERT OR IGNORE (matching the DM path at line 977 which correctly checks before inserting). This differs from handle_follow which uses INSERT OR IGNORE."}
